blocks.text: DTC P0401: EGR valve flow abnormality

The EGR valve is controlled by the PGM-FI ECU; when the EGR valve opens, some of the exhaust gas flows through the exhaust gas recirculation system into the intake manifold. The exhaust gases mix with the air mixture and are then drawn into the combustion chamber to lower the combustion temperature and reduce NOx emissions. EGR valve flow failure is judged as follows. The EGR valve is fully closed during deceleration with the throttle valve fully closed. The PGM-FI ECU then opens the EGR valve fully; after a set time, the PGM-FI ECU detects fluctuations in intake manifold pressure when the EGR valve is fully opened to fully closed, and calculates the ratio of actual EGR flow to the normal EGR flow predicted by the PGM-FI ECU. If the ratio of the actual EGR flow rate is less than the set value, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
Ratio of actual EGR flow rate to estimated normal EGR flow rate is less than 30% for more than 3.5 seconds.
Judgment is made by the ratio of the measured pressure fluctuation to the reference value of the intake manifold pressure fluctuation at the preset normal EGR flow rate.

items.text: Start the engine, run the radiator fan twice, and then warm up the engine for at least 2 minutes at 3,000 rpm with no load.
Vehicle runs at a speed of 24-100 km/h for at least 5 seconds.
Vehicle speed [vehicle speed] 90-100 km/h for at least 10 seconds at a constant speed.
Decelerate with the throttle valve fully closed for at least 4 seconds.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
